additionalInfo:
CONSORTIUM STRUCTURE:

MAIN PARTICIPANT (SME): ALD Engineering & Construction LLC
- Legal form: Limited Liability Company (ТОВ), registered in Ukraine
- Employees: 219 (qualifies as SME under EU definition: <250 employees)
- Core business: Construction engineering, cost estimation, procurement management
- Proprietary ERP system serving construction industry
- SaaS business model for construction documentation services
- 6 YEARS OF PROPRIETARY PROCUREMENT DATA: structured database covering cost estimates, material prices, supplier data, technical specifications, project documentation across Ukrainian construction market
- PIC code: REGISTRATION REQUIRED (urgent, deadline Feb 25, 2026)

SUPPORTING PARTICIPANT: Zaporizhzhia National University (ZNU) - Laboratory of Parallel and Distributed Computing
- Head: Dr. Halyna Shylo, Doctor of Technical Sciences
- Team: 4 researchers (1 PhD, 1 Professor)
- Expertise: HPC, Parallel Computing, AI/ML, Distributed Systems
- Role: AI model architecture design, HPC optimization, training pipeline engineering, model evaluation
- Publications: 25 total (8 in last year), Scopus-indexed
- Current project: AI in logistics systems for national security (3.59M UAH state budget)
- International partnerships: Durham University, TU Bergakademie Freiberg

PROJECT: CosTERRA — Cognitive System for Technical Engineering, Research & Architectural documentation
A domain-specific generative AI model for automated generation of construction cost estimation documents.

KEY DATA ASSET:
- 6 years of structured procurement data (cost estimates, BOQs, material specifications)
- Thousands of completed cost estimation documents
- Domain-specific terminology and standards (Ukrainian construction norms: DBN, DSTU)
- Price dynamics, supplier databases, regional cost variations
- Project templates and standardized documentation formats

WHY CUSTOM GENERATIVE MODEL IS NECESSARY:
1. Construction cost estimation documents follow strict domain-specific formats and standards
2. General-purpose LLMs lack knowledge of Ukrainian construction norms (DBN, DSTU)
3. Accurate cost estimation requires understanding of regional price dynamics and material specifications
4. Existing models cannot generate compliant technical documentation with correct calculations
5. Proprietary data contains unique patterns and domain knowledge not available in public training corpora

supportingDocuments.description: Ukraine officially associated with Digital Europe Programme for supercomputing, AI, and digital skills.
Ukraine listed among eligible countries for EuroHPC supercomputers access.
FFplus eligibility: organizations in EU Member States OR countries associated with Digital Europe Programme (Ukraine included).
